consistency.notes: The spell level (2) is consistent with typical mid-level utility spells. The school (Abjuration) aligns with the protective/locking nature of the effect.

quality.notes: All standard spell fields (slug, name, level, school, ritual, casting time, range, components, duration, description, classes) are present and well-defined.

mechanics.core_effect: Magically locks a non-magical or already locked object, requiring specific interaction (password or designated creature) to open normally.
interaction_points: Designated creatures can open it normally.
A spoken password (within 5ft) suppresses the effect for 1 minute.
The *Knock* spell suppresses it for 10 minutes.
The object becomes significantly harder to force open (DC +10 to break/pick locks).
mechanics.resource_cost: Material component (gold dust worth at least 25 gp, consumed). This is a significant cost for a 2nd-level spell, reinforcing the permanent-like nature of the lock.
mechanics.balance_assessment: Mechanically strong for utility and defense. The consumed 25gp material cost balances the very strong, long-duration lockdown effect. The interaction rules are clear and provide countermeasures.
